neighborhoods.name: Old Town / Downtown
neighborhoods.description: Historic Spanish colonial plaza with adobe buildings, museums, and the vibrant Route 66 corridor

housing.predominantType: Adobe houses, lofts, and converted historic buildings

neighborhoods.character_notes: The historic and commercial heart of Albuquerque. Most walkable area with best access to cultural attractions, restaurants, and public services.

neighborhoods.name: Nob Hill / University
neighborhoods.description: Walkable district along Route 66 with UNM campus, independent shops, and craft breweries
neighborhoods.character: Walkable university, Route 66 shops, craft beer

housing.predominantType: Bungalows, ranch houses, and small apartments

neighborhoods.character_notes: Established residential area offering a quieter lifestyle while maintaining reasonable access to Albuquerque's center and services.

neighborhoods.name: Southwest Mesa / Rio Rancho
neighborhoods.description: Western suburbs with newer development, desert views, and significantly lower housing costs

housing.predominantType: Newer tract homes and affordable apartments

neighborhoods.character_notes: More affordable option for budget-conscious retirees. May require a car or longer transit times to reach central amenities.

neighborhoods.name: Northeast Heights / Sandia Foothills
neighborhoods.description: Upscale eastern area against the Sandia Mountains with hiking access, good schools, and mountain views

housing.predominantType: Ranch houses, custom adobes, and mountain-view homes

neighborhoods.character_notes: Family-friendly area with good schools, parks, and community amenities. Popular with active retirees and families.
